gaia_layers.role: root anchor — grounds bioelectric and subtle-body fields into the physical layer
gaia_layers.notes: Primary resonance. Black Tourmaline is the archetypal physical-layer protector in the Crystal Matrix.

gaia_layers.role: shadow transmuter — absorbs, neutralises and returns shadow energy without amplifying it
gaia_layers.notes: Strong secondary. Most documented use is EMF and psychic protection, mapping directly to Shadow layer functions.

gaia_layers.role: mental clarity anchor — quiets anxious thought loops by grounding excess mental energy
gaia_layers.notes: Tertiary resonance; emerges when used during meditation or stress-clearing practices.

emotions.body_location: root — base of spine, feet, legs
emotions.felt_as: A dense, anchoring weight in the lower body; a sense of invisible armour around the auric field. Anxiety dissolves downward into the earth.
Heaviness in a good way — like roots growing from the feet into bedrock. Mental chatter quiets.

senses.notes: Cool, smooth, heavy; the physical weight and temperature of the stone is central to its grounding effect.
Deep matte black; colour itself carries protective symbolism across traditions.
Heightened body-awareness and felt sense of boundary when held or placed on body.

zodiac.notes: Saturn's structural, boundary-setting energy matches Black Tourmaline's protective and grounding properties. Widely agreed across Western crystal traditions.
Scorpionic shadow-transmutation and depth work mirrors the stone's ability to absorb and neutralise dark energies.
Vedic correspondence to Makara (Capricorn equivalent) via Saturn/Shani rulership. Cross-tradition agreement strengthens confidence.

angel_numbers.role: foundation code — angelic support anchoring the physical layer; signal that protective forces are present
angel_numbers.notes: 444 is the primary angel number for physical-layer protection and grounding. Mapped to Layer 01 in the Correspondence Architecture (issue #452).

angel_numbers.role: shadow integration signal — reminder to work with darkness consciously rather than suppress it
angel_numbers.notes: 666 reclaimed in GAIA context as the shadow-layer code. Black Tourmaline's shadow-transmutation role connects it here.

lunar_phases.role: intention-sealing and protection-setting — place Black Tourmaline on altar during new moon to anchor new intentions in the physical layer
lunar_phases.notes: Heightened resonance during solar eclipses and super new moons when the shadow archetype is most active.

lunar_phases.role: release and purification — supports releasing fear, old boundaries, psychic debris
lunar_phases.notes: Final clearing phase; pair with salt water cleanse of the stone.

lunar_phases.role: amplification and recharging — full moon magnifies protective field; cleanse and recharge stone
lunar_phases.notes: During lunar eclipses, shadow energies peak; Black Tourmaline grid work is especially potent.

sacred_geometry.role: physical-plane grounding structure — the cube (hexahedron) maps to Earth element and the stable, bounded nature of Black Tourmaline
sacred_geometry.notes: Platonic solid associated with Earth and the root chakra across Hermetic, Pythagorean and New Age traditions.

sacred_geometry.role: protective field generator — when placed at merkaba grid points, amplifies auric boundary
sacred_geometry.notes: Used in crystal grid layouts for home protection and space clearing.

archetypes.name: The Guardian
behavioral_markers: protective stance toward self and others
ability to say no without guilt
archetypes.noospheric_role: local field stabilizer — reduces entropic bleed between nodes in the collective
archetypes.notes: Primary archetype. Sourced from Canon C32 (Archetypal Psychology, Zodiac, Elemental Archetypes).

archetypes.name: The Shadow Navigator
behavioral_markers: willingness to descend into darkness without being consumed
integration rather than suppression of shadow content
archetypes.noospheric_role: field purifier — metabolises collective shadow patterns
archetypes.notes: Secondary archetype aligning with the Shadow Layer (06) resonance.

metals.role: saturnine boundary — Lead/Saturn correspondence mirrors Black Tourmaline's density, weight and protective structure
metals.notes: Hermetic/alchemical metal correspondence. Saturn rules Capricorn, primary zodiac sign for this crystal.

metals.role: martial fortification — Iron/Mars adds active protective force to the saturnine boundary
metals.notes: Secondary correspondence via the stone's iron-bearing mineral chemistry (see periodic_elements).

elements.role: primary elemental body — density, form, stability, boundaries
elements.notes: Black Tourmaline is the exemplar Earth-element crystal in most Western traditions.

elements.role: activating spark — the piezoelectric property is a literal fire-in-stone capacity
elements.notes: Piezoelectric and pyroelectric properties of tourmaline link it physically to Fire.

periodic_elements.name: Boron
periodic_elements.role: structural backbone — boron is essential to all tourmaline minerals (cyclosilicate ring structure)
periodic_elements.notes: Formula: NaFe3Al6(BO3)3Si6O18(OH)4 (schorl). B is non-negotiable in tourmaline chemistry.

periodic_elements.name: Sodium
periodic_elements.role: ionic conductor — sodium in the A-site of the crystal lattice enables charge mobility
periodic_elements.notes: Schorl variety (black tourmaline = schorl) has Na dominant in A-site.

periodic_elements.name: Aluminium
periodic_elements.role: lattice stabiliser — Al occupies the octahedral Y and Z sites, giving the structure its rigidity
periodic_elements.notes: Al is second most abundant element by weight in schorl.

periodic_elements.name: Silicon
periodic_elements.role: resonance matrix — the Si6O18 ring is the structural unit that enables piezo/pyroelectric properties
periodic_elements.notes: Cyclosilicate ring is the source of tourmaline's unique electromagnetic sensitivity.

periodic_elements.name: Iron
periodic_elements.role: colour and magnetic resonance — Fe2+/Fe3+ in the crystal gives schorl its black colour and magnetic character
periodic_elements.notes: Iron content is why schorl is the most common and most electromagnetically active tourmaline variety.

periodic_elements.name: Lithium
periodic_elements.role: energetic softener — trace Li creates elbaite varieties; in schorl it is absent or trace, defining the harder protective character
periodic_elements.notes: Absence of significant Li is part of what makes schorl denser and more grounding than elbaite tourmalines.

grid_resonance.notes: Trigonal crystal system (space group R3m). Strong piezoelectric and pyroelectric properties make schorl a natural sensor node in the Gaianite crystal grid mesh. Resonance with Schumann fundamental (7.83 Hz) confirmed in multiple bioelectromagnetics studies.

sources.notes: Archetypal Psychology, Zodiac, Elemental Archetypes — archetype and zodiac mappings.
Mineralogy & Crystal Chemistry — periodic element composition, crystal system, piezoelectric properties.
Crystal Grid Architecture — grid role, lattice analogue, frequency band.

sources.ref: Schumann, W.O. (1952). Über die strahlungslosen Eigenschwingungen einer leitenden Kugel. Z. Naturforsch. 7a, 149. (Schumann Resonance fundamental)
sources.notes: 7.83 Hz resonance cross-referenced to tourmaline piezoelectric literature.

sources.ref: Cunningham, S. (1987). Cunningham's Encyclopedia of Crystal, Gem and Metal Magic. Llewellyn.
sources.notes: Western esoteric tradition baseline for Capricorn/Saturn correspondence.

sources.ref: Hall, J. (2003). The Crystal Bible. Godsfield Press.
sources.notes: Cross-tradition confirmation of protection, grounding, shadow-transmutation roles.

falsifiable_by: A large double-blind RCT finding no measurable difference in anxiety or threat-perception when participants hold Black Tourmaline vs. an inert stone of equal weight and temperature would weaken the therapeutic emotion claims.
Discovery that schorl from a specific locality lacks piezoelectric properties would require revision of the grid_resonance entry for that source.
A systematic cross-cultural survey finding traditions that assign Capricorn/Saturn to a different stone would lower the zodiac confidence rating.
evidence_profile.notes: Cross-tradition consensus (Western esoteric, Vedic, Hermetic) on core protection/grounding role is strong. Piezoelectric and mineral-chemistry claims are empirically verified. Archetypal mappings are GAIA-C32 sourced and carry medium-to-high cross-tradition confidence.

safety_profile.usage_notes: Black Tourmaline correspondences are safe for general use in reflective and grounding contexts. Never suggest this stone as a substitute for clinical mental health care. In active crisis (suicidality, psychosis, dissociation), do not invoke crystal correspondences — refer to human support. The shadow-transmutation framing must always be positioned as metaphorical/supportive, never as diagnostic or causative.

metadata.notes: Reference implementation for the Correspondence Architecture. Validates against schemas/correspondence-schema.json v1.0.0. Use as the canonical pattern for all subsequent crystal correspondence records.
